# Link QR Wallet

> Android app that turns URLs into QR codes and stores them in a searchable, sortable offline library.

## What it does

Link QR Wallet lets users save links as QR codes for quick access. Add a URL and the app auto-fetches the page title; a QR code is generated and stored locally. Users can search and sort their library, scan existing QR codes to import them, and share QR images. Everything is stored offline — no account or cloud sync required.

## Key features

- Add links with auto-fetched page titles
- Generate and display QR codes for any URL
- QR scanner to import existing codes (CameraX + ML Kit)
- Search and sort by title, domain, or date
- Share QR images via Android share intent
- Local database (Room/SQLite), fully offline-first
- No account, no cloud, no tracking

## Architecture

- Language: Kotlin
- Framework: Jetpack Compose, Room, CameraX, ML Kit, ZXing
- Architecture: Clean Architecture + MVVM
- Build tool: Gradle (Kotlin DSL)
- Min SDK: Android 7.0 (API 24)

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/cocodedk/LinkQRWallet
- Website: https://cocodedk.github.io/LinkQRWallet/
